data.description:
## What

The centralized resolver `rebar._engine_support.resolver.resolve_ticket_id` accepts full IDs, 8-hex short IDs, aliases, and unique prefixes, and is the single seam used by the library (`__init__.py`), `signing.py`, all `_commands`, `_engine_support` reads, the reducer link-resolution path, and plan-review. It tries to resolve Jira keys via `_scan_alias_jira`, which matches `data.jira_key` on CREATE events — but the reconciler never writes that field. The authoritative Jira<->rebar mapping lives in the binding store reverse index at `.tickets-tracker/.bridge_state/bindings.json` (`reverse: {jira_key -> local_id}`). Result: `rebar show REB-310` (and every other surface) fails to resolve a Jira key even though the binding exists.

## Why

Jira IDs (e.g. `REB-310`) are the IDs humans and the Jira side use. They should resolve to the bound local ticket the same way aliases and short IDs do, through the one centralized resolver, so the fix covers `show`/`claim`/`transition`/`link`/library uniformly.

## Approach

In `src/rebar/_engine_support/resolver.py`:
- Add a Jira-key shape regex (`^[A-Za-z][A-Za-z0-9]*-[0-9]+$`) — distinct from lowercase-hex IDs/short-IDs and lowercase-word aliases, so no collision.
- Add a best-effort helper `_resolve_via_binding_store(target, tracker_dir)` that reads `<tracker_dir>/.bridge_state/bindings.json`, looks up `reverse[target]` (and `target.upper()`), and returns the local ticket-dir name if it exists on disk; missing/corrupt store -> None (Jira resolution simply unavailable, never raises).
- In `resolve_ticket_id`, for Jira-key-shaped input consult the binding store first, before the alias/jira field scan and prefix fallback. Keep the legacy `_scan_alias_jira` jira_key-field path as a harmless fallback.

## Scope

Resolution only (input -> ticket). Display/formatting of a Jira key in `lookups.py::_auto` reads `data.jira_key` too and is also effectively dead, but is a separate concern (out of scope; capture as discovered follow-up if confirmed).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `resolve_ticket_id("REB-310", tracker)` returns the bound local ticket dir when `.bridge_state/bindings.json` reverse-maps it and the dir exists.
- [ ] A Jira-shaped input with no binding (or a missing/corrupt bindings.json) returns None without raising and without false-matching an alias/prefix.
- [ ] Existing alias / short-id / full-id / prefix resolution is unchanged (no regression).
- [ ] Unit tests cover: bound key resolves; unbound key -> None; missing bindings.json -> None; corrupt bindings.json -> None; binding points at a non-existent dir -> None.
- [ ] `rebar show REB-310` resolves end-to-end (manual check noted on the ticket).
- [ ] make lint + the resolver test module pass.
data.title: Resolve Jira issue keys (REB-NNN) in the centralized ID resolver via the binding store
